title: Betrayal Black Turns Luffy Away
description: On pages 5–7, betrayal black turns luffy away as miss Goldenweek turns Luffy’s emotions against him while Vivi, Nami, and Zoro remain trapped in Mr. 3’s deadly wax display.
scene: Betrayal Black Turns Luffy Away. Miss Goldenweek turns Luffy’s emotions against him while Vivi, Nami, and Zoro remain trapped in Mr. 3’s deadly wax display. The focus is the story beat, character decisions, and dialogue driving this part.

dialogue.speaker: Miss Goldenweek
dialogue.text: Touch the black painting and you'll want to betray your friends no matter how important they are to you.

dialogue.speaker: Vivi
dialogue.text: is a realist painter who can realistically portray even the very colour of emotions.

dialogue.speaker: Miss Goldenweek
dialogue.text: Her special paintings, when touched by a person,

dialogue.speaker: Vivi
dialogue.text: Has the special power to suggest various emotions to the subconscious.

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: suggestions to the sub- conscious!? Oh crap!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: For a simple-minded idiot like him,

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Hypnosis or some other mind tricks,

dialogue.speaker: Miss Goldenweek
dialogue.text: Are the most effective attacks you can use!

dialogue.speaker: Miss Goldenweek
dialogue.text: But... then all we have to do is get him off of that weird painting, right!?

dialogue.speaker: Miss Valentine
dialogue.text: Luffy! Get away from that spot!

dialogue.speaker: Mr. 5
dialogue.text: You mustn't step one foot outside from the black symbol beneath your feet! Please!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: You to help us at all!

dialogue.speaker: Miss Valentine
dialogue.text: I... I just felt really weird all of a sudden!

dialogue.speaker: Miss Valentine
dialogue.text: I'm coming to help you right now!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: But before that, I gotta laugh! Aha- hahahaha!

summary: This part centers on betrayal black turns luffy away, clarifying the immediate conflict and how it moves the chapter toward its next major turn.
